channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Lagos APC candidate promises reforms
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos APC candidate, Oluwagbenga Abiola, pledges responsive representation and reforms in youth empowerment, education, and healthcare for Agege Constituen
Read More: https://punchng.com/lagos-apc-candidate-promises-reforms/

item.title: Fraud: Anambra native doctor jailed six years
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
An Anambra State High Court has sentenced popular native doctor Onyeze Jesus to six years in prison for fraud, misleading the public with supernatural weal
Read More: https://punchng.com/fraud-anambra-native-doctor-jailed-six-years/

item.title: Navy hands 756kg of seized cannabis to NDLEA
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Nigerian Navy Ship BEECROFT has seized 756kg of cannabis sativa in intelligence-led operations and handed it over to the NDLEA, reaffirming commitment
Read More: https://punchng.com/navy-hands-756kg-of-seized-cannabis-to-ndlea/

item.title: Nigerian arrested over cyber fraud in India
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Nigerian national has been arrested in India for alleged involvement in a N250m cyber fraud, defrauding a retired official. Get details on the Nigerian c
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerian-arrested-over-cyber-fraud-in-india/

item.title: Tinubu signs crypto, virtual assets regulation order
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu signs an Executive Order to regulate crypto and virtual assets in Nigeria, strengthening oversight and protecting investors from financial
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-signs-crypto-virtual-assets-regulation-order/

item.title: Emefiele finally forfeits mansions, $2m to FG
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Supreme Court has affirmed the final forfeiture of Godwin Emefiele’s mansions, $2.045 million, and share certificates to the Federal Government.
Read More: https://punchng.com/emefiele-finally-forfeits-mansions-2m-to-fg/

item.title: Osun 2026: NPC flags 15 LGs over violence
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The National Peace Committee identifies 15 Osun LGAs, including Osogbo and Irewole, as high-risk for election violence ahead of the 2026 governorship elect
Read More: https://punchng.com/osun-2026-npc-flags-15-lgs-over-violence/

item.title: Backlash as FG renames sixth facility after Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government’s decision to rename the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway after President Bola Tinubu sparks backlash. Critics decry the Tinubu facilit
Read More: https://punchng.com/backlash-as-fg-renames-sixth-facility-after-tinubu/

item.title: FG renews drive to eradicate livestock disease by 2030
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s Federal Government reaffirms its commitment to eradicate Peste des Petits Ruminants (PPR), a devastating livestock disease, by 2030. Learn about
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-renews-drive-to-eradicate-livestock-disease-by-2030/

item.title: Nigerian qualifications top fraud alerts in UK – Report
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A new report shows Nigerian qualifications have the highest fraud alert rate among international student applications to UK universities.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerian-qualifications-top-fraud-alerts-in-uk-report/

item.title: CWG: NSC raises athlete benefits as Amusan storms camp
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Team Nigeria has received a major morale boost ahead of the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, with the National Sports Commission (NSC) unveiling an enhanced welfare and incentive package aimed at ensuring the country’s athletes compete under improved conditions. The post CWG: NSC raises athlete b

item.title: WAFU B U20 Tourney: Flying Eagles to begin title defence against Satellites in Yamoussoukro
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria’s U20 boys, Flying Eagles, will kick off their WAFU B title defence against the Black Satellites of Ghana on 27th July at the prestigious Lycée Scientifique de Yamoussoukro in Côte d’Ivoire. The post WAFU B U20 Tourney: Flying Eagles to begin title defence against Satellites in Yamoussoukro

item.title: Iheanacho agrees deal with Bursaspor
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Super Eagles striker Kelechi Iheanacho has reportedly reached an agreement to join Turkish club Bursaspor after his contract with Scottish champions Celtic expired at the end of the 2025/2026 season. The post Iheanacho agrees deal with Bursaspor appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Fenerbahce joins race for Onuachu
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Turkish Super Lig giants Fenerbahce have joined the race to sign Nigeria international Paul Onuachu, BSNSports.com.ng reports. The post Fenerbahce joins race for Onuachu appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Nigeria qualifies for 2026 African Nations Championship
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria have qualified for the 2026 African Nations Championship after extending their unbeaten run at the Zone 3 qualifiers in Abidjan, Ivory Coast, BSNSports.com.ng reports. The post Nigeria qualifies for 2026 African Nations Championship appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Joshua sets sight on Prenga tune-up fight July 25
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Anthony Joshua is on a collision course with Tyson Fury, but he will have to get through Kristian Prenga first. The post Joshua sets sight on Prenga tune-up fight July 25 appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Usman vs. Plessis: Former UFC champions meet in crucial main event
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: “The Nigerian Nightmare” Kamaru Usman gets one of the biggest tests of his late-career run on Saturday, July 18, when he meets former UFC middleweight champion Dricus du Plessis in the five-round main event of UFC Fight Night 281 at Paycom Center in Oklahoma City. The bout is set at 185 pounds and p

item.title: The Green Covenant: Why Globacom’s partnership with dealers is the living architecture of Nigeria’s prosperity
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: By Hamza Arab Kabiru In every thriving economy, there exists an unseen architecture more enduring than steel, more resilient than concrete, and more valuable than the wealth it ultimately creates. It is the architecture of trust. In Nigeria’s telecommunications industry, this invisible cathedral has

item.title: FIFA, NFF workshop boost for women administrators –Sanusi
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Nigeria Football Federation (NFF) has reaffirmed its commitment to advancing women’s football administration with the commencement of a three-day FIFA capacity-building workshop for women football administrators in Abuja. The post FIFA, NFF workshop boost for women administrators –Sanusi appeare

item.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

item.title: 92 Years of Soyinkitude
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: He is greatness personified. At 92 on Monday this week, our sole Nobel laureate, Professor Wole Soyinka, has become the embodiment of everyone’s idea of a father, grandfather and great-grandfather. The post 92 Years of Soyinkitude appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: The World Cup–winning side will make $50 million — and the IRS gets a cut
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “It doesn’t make a difference who wins the game. The IRS will get a piece.”

item.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

item.description: Shares in SpaceX declined about 4% in premarket trading on Friday after aborted mission.

item.description: Pressure is growing within the EU to sanction Israel for its illegal settlements and violence against Palestinians.

item.title: Trump threatens Canada with steeper tariff costs over wildfire smoke
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US president has frequently pushed false claims blaming the mismanagement of rival politicians for large wildfires.

item.title: Lagos Re-Arraigns Woman For Failing To Stop Late Husband’s Fake Medical Practice
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Lagos State government has re-arraigned a widow, Kafayat Oyelakin, before the state high court sitting in Ikeja for allegedly failing to stop her late husband from practising medicine despite knowing that he was not a qualified medical doctor. Oyelakin was re-arraigned before Justice Oyindamola

item.title: FG, Partners Move To Strengthen Local Drug Manufacturing For Health Security
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government and its development partners have renewed their commitment to strengthening local pharmaceutical manufacturing, describing domestic production of medicines as critical to Nigeria’s health security, economic growth and resilience against future public health emergencies.

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Moonshot AI’s new open model is catching up to Anthropic and OpenAI, raising questions about the future of the AI race.

item.title: Rescued Oyo Teacher Says Kidnappers Blindfolded Victims, Banned Prayers In Jesus’ Name
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: One of the teachers rescued after spending 56 days in the custody of kidnappers in Oyo State, Zacchaeus Olatunde, has recounted the harrowing ordeal, revealing that the captives were permanently blindfolded, barred from praying in the name of Jesus for weeks and lived each day uncertain whether they

item.title: NRC declares war on railway vandals
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The recovered railway components, estimated to be worth about ₦200 million, were intercepted during an intelligence-led operation, a development the NRC boss described as a major breakthrough in the campaign against the destruction of railway infrastructure.

item.title: PCN Seals 505 Drug Outlets In Cross River Over Regulatory Violations
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Pharmacy Council of Nigeria (PCN) has sealed 505 pharmaceutical premises in Cross River State following a four-day enforcement exercise across 10 local government areas. PCN Director of Enforcement, Dr. Suleiman Chiroma, disclosed this on Friday in Calabar, saying inspectors visited 602 premises

item.title: Iraq signs deals with Western oil firms, including to revive Syria pipeline
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Deal with Chevron aims to rebuild a crude oil pipeline that runs from Iraq's oil-rich Kirkuk to Syria's Baniyas.

item.title: Gabon forward Aubameyang leaves Marseille for Deportivo La Coruna
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Aubameyang, who still had a year remaining on his contract with the Ligue 1 club, leaves after playing 92 matches and scoring 44 goals. The post Gabon forward Aubameyang leaves Marseille for Deportivo La Coruna appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to her, balancing her private life with her public image was a gradual process that involved making mistakes, learning from them and growing into a better version of herself.

item.description: Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, on Friday declared that the massive infrastructure projects executed under President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s administration have effectively silenced political critics, insisting that tangible performance remains the best response to op

item.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

item.description: Fresh internal crisis has rocked the Cross River State chapter of the Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, following the emergence of a new governorship candidate on Friday. The new candidate, Julius Udayi Esq., who presented himself at the NUJ Secretariat in Calabar, claimed to be the authentic flag bear

item.description: The US military says it's degrading Iran's military but has been accused by Tehran of targeting civilian infrastructure.

item.title: Baker Tilly Eyes $3 Billion in Debt to Replace Private Credit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG is preparing to sell about $3 billion in debt markets for accounting services firm Baker Tilly Advisory Group to refinance private credit loans, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce from the US market after cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Food producer Taylor Farms released a statement on the Cyclospora outbreak Friday, confirming that it's "voluntarily removing all iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico from the US market." Reuters reports that, according to a source, Taylor Farms told customers like Yum Brands owner Taco Bell

item.title: Microfinance Bank Employee Arraigned In Lagos Over Alleged Forgery
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A 37-year-old employee of a microfinance bank, Henry Nwoko, was on Friday arraigned before an Ejigbo Magistrates’ Court in Lagos over the alleged forgery of a letter of non-indebtedness. Nwoko is facing a two-count charge bordering on forgery and conduct likely to cause a breach of peace. He, howeve

item.title: IGP Disu Vows Police Will Hunt Down Kidnappers, Rescue All Abducted Victims
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The inspector-general of police (IGP), Olatunji Rilwan Disu, has reaffirmed the Nigeria Police Force’s commitment to pursuing kidnappers across the country and securing the release of every kidnapped victim, declaring that security operatives will not relent until criminal gangs are dismantled

item.title: Accord Party Leaders Pass Vote Of Confidence In National Chairman Mgbudem
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Leaders of the Accord Party have passed a vote of confidence in the party’s National Chairman, Barrister Maxwell Mgbudem, commending his leadership and describing it as visionary, purposeful and transformative. The endorsement came during a solidarity visit by the party’s state chairmen

item.title: Police gun down kidnapper, rescue victim in Kogi
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The security team engaged the abductors in a fierce gun battle, during which one of the suspected kidnappers was fatally shot, forcing the remaining gang members to flee into the forest and abandon their captive.

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Seven of the worst performers for the month were still up by triple digits for 2026.

item.title: Gauge of Oracle’s Credit Risk Hits Fresh All Time High
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A measure of Oracle Corp.’s credit risk hit a record high on Friday as concerns grew about both the company’s aggressive cash-burn and the future profitability of its artificial intelligence investments amid a new Chinese model that could rival offerings from companies like OpenAI.

item.description: Ikorodu City have completed the signing of midfielder Adam Abdulraman, DAILY POST reports. Abdulraman joined the Oga Boys from Abuja-based Mahanaim FC. item.dc:creator: New Telegraph

item.title: Transfer: Super Eagles winger swaps club in Russia
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Super Eagles winger Olakunle Olusegun has sealed a permanent transfer to Russian Premier League outfit FC Rostov, DAILY POST reports. item.title: Benue community holds mass burial for victims of deadly attack
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Residents of Otukpo-Nobi community in Otukpo Local Government Area of Benue State on Friday held a mass burial for victims killed in last Sunday’s attack by suspected armed herders. The solemn ceremony drew grieving families, traditional rulers, religious leaders, community members and sympath

item.title: Flooding: Why Tinubu should declare state of emergency on gully erosion in South-East – Omeire
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The senatorial candidate of Imo West of the Action Peoples Party in the coming 2027 election, Rufus Omeire, has explained why President Bola Ahmed Tinubu should declare a state of emergency on gully erosion in the South-East region.  He made this known in a statement on Friday.  Omeir

item.title: Topcoat: The full full-stack framework for Rust
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/tokio-rs/topcoat Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48952067 Points: 20 # Comments: 9

item.title: Benue Chief Judge warns magistrates against corruption
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …Says Judicial Compromise Erodes Public Confidence in Justice System By Peter Duru, Makurdi The Chief Judge of Benue State, Hon. Justice Maurice Ahemba Ikpambese, has cautioned magistrates in the state against all forms of corruption, warning that any compromise of judicial independence undermines p

item.title: Tuchel urges England to finish strongly in emotional address
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel urges his players to end the World Cup strongly in an emotional address after their agonising semi-final defeat by Argentina.

item.title: Done Deal: Super Eagles Winger Completes Russian Club Switch
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Russian Premier League FC Rostov have completed the signing of Super Eagles winger Olakunle Olusegun, Completesports.com reports. Olusegun linked up with FC Rostov from another Russian club, Krasnodar. The former Golden Eaglets star spent last season on loan at Nizhny Novgorod, scoring five times in

item.title: AI Upheaval Crushes Retail Traders Crowding Into Leveraged Funds
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A surprise breakthrough from Chinese startup Moonshot rippled through global markets Friday, sending AI and semiconductor stocks sharply lower and reviving memories of last year’s so-called DeepSeek moment. It was the latest reminder that markets are being reshaped at extraordinary speed and that as

item.title: Dangote Refinery raises $2.5 billion in private placement ahead of planned IPO
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria's Dangote Petroleum Refinery has raised $2.5 billion through a private placement as it prepares for a planned initial public offering (IPO) later this year, strengthening its financing position ahead of its next phase of expansion. The post Dangote Refinery raises $2.5 billion in private pla
